scores.brief_feedback: Strong practical measures with ethical grounding (Yatha Raja, whistleblower protection). Slightly exceeds word limit (180 vs 150); presentation clarity could be tightened. Excellent content accuracy and logical structure.

Excellent breadth using Triple Bottom Line framework and Indian philosophies (Antyodaya, Sarvodaya). Well-structured addressing food, energy, environment, and instability. Exceeds word limit (180 vs 150) but demonstrates sophisticated ethical reasoning.

Comprehensive case analysis with all four parts addressed. Strong ethical reasoning (Nishkama Karma, utilitarianism). Slightly exceeds word limit (311 vs 250). Could deepen analysis of emotions and leadership credibility impact.

Excellent interdisciplinary analysis addressing false dichotomy fallacy, intergenerational equity, and SDG linkage. Three practical alternatives provided. Exceeds word limit (311 vs 250); strong structure and policy integration.

Strong identification of conflict of interest, nepotism, breach of confidentiality, and quid pro quo. Four options with pros/cons. Well-reasoned choice of Option 3 with proactive transparency. Exceeds word limit significantly (345 vs 250).

Nuanced exploration of probity vs. ambition dilemma. Balanced approach combining Option 2 and 1 demonstrates emotional intelligence and institutional knowledge. Significantly exceeds word limit (374 vs 250). Content strong but length impacts presentation score.

Comprehensive administrative response with specific remedial measures (ABPS, DBT, NMMS, geo-tagging). Good integration of technology with accountability mechanisms. Exceeds word limit (337 vs 250) but demonstrates strong systems thinking.

Strong resolution of humanitarian vs. security dilemma with Option 3 (conditional entry). Good address of legal frameworks (Foreigners Act, Article 21, non-refoulement). Slightly exceeds word limit (303 vs 250); border security precautions well-explained.
